findings.title: Minor #2 (round 1) — CorruptionException not caught — CONFIRMED false positive, no action required
findings.description: Round 1 flagged that catch (e: IOException) in DefaultAppSettingsRepository would miss androidx.datastore.core.CorruptionException. Bytecode inspection of datastore-core-android 1.1.7 (classes.jar, javap output) confirms: CorruptionException extends java.io.IOException (super_class: java/io/IOException). The existing catch block therefore covers CorruptionException by inheritance. The deferred disposition is correct.
findings.impact: None — no exploitable gap. The catch block covers all DataStore-level IO and corruption exceptions.
findings.recommendation: No change required. Optionally add an inline comment referencing the verified inheritance for future maintainers.

findings.title: Minor #3 (round 1) — modelId unbounded — CONFIRMED deferred, no escalation
findings.description: modelId is sourced exclusively from the bundled model allowlist (internal trust boundary). No user-provided input reaches this parameter in the current codebase. The proto map key encoding is binary-safe with no injection risk. ErrorLog already truncates modelId to 500 chars in log output. The deferred disposition (re-evaluate if Phase 4 Projects introduces user-provided keys) remains appropriate.
findings.impact: Theoretical latent DoS via key size bloat only if a future module passes an externally-derived modelId. No current exposure.
findings.recommendation: No change required now. Add a precondition guard (require(modelId.length <= 512)) when Phase 4 Projects introduces user-configurable keys that flow into this API.

round2_verification.cve_scope: All com.google.protobuf < 4.27.2 on 4.x branch. Fix threshold: 4.27.2+.
round2_verification.commit_verified: 127b381 — single-line diff, clean bump, no other changes to protobuf configuration
round2_verification.gallery_source_note: gallery-source/Android/src/gradle/libs.versions.toml still references protobufJavaLite 4.26.1 but gallery-source is not included in settings.gradle.kts (:app, :core-runtime, :core-settings only). It is an upstream read-only reference copy and does not affect the app build graph. No action required.
round2_verification.new_findings_from_bump: None. The 4.26 -> 4.28 protobuf API surface used by the project (AppSettings.parseFrom, AppSettings.getDefaultInstance, toBuilder, putPerModelOverrides, removePerModelOverrides, writeTo) is stable across this range. AppSettingsSerializer compiles and all 6 tests pass (per commit message). No new CVEs in protobuf 4.28.x as of 2026-04-16.
round2_verification.hardcoded_secrets_scan: Clean — no secrets, tokens, passwords, or credentials found in core-settings source or gradle configuration.
round2_verification.other_owasp_checks: All other OWASP Top 10 checks from round 1 remain valid: DataStore file is app-private (A01), no PII in logs (A09), no network or IPC proto parsing surface (A10/A03), backup disabled (A05), Hilt singleton correct (A04). No regressions introduced by commit 127b381.
